p1.sub: End-to-end data pipeline connecting MotherDuck cloud warehouse via DuckDB, cleaning 1.6M job postings, producing 12 Plotly visualisations, and training a salary regression model — R² 0.35 on US market data.
p1.s1_sub: Median annual salary by job title — US market, 2023–2025
p1.s2_sub: Monthly job postings volume and remote-work share, Jan 2023 – Nov 2024
p1.s3_sub: Top in-demand skills and leading countries by posting volume
p1.s4_sub: Linear Regression on log-transformed salary — US jobs only
p1.ml_note: Why log-transform? Salary skewness was 1.272 (right-tailed). Log-transforming the target normalises the distribution, making linear regression work correctly. Predictions are exponentiated back at inference time. Ridge vs Plain Linear: both scored R²≈0.35 — regularisation wasn't needed at this feature count.

bio.intro: Former game developer with hands-on experience building interactive systems and shipping titles. After years working in game production, I made the pivot to data analytics — applying the same systems-thinking and performance-obsession mindset to uncovering insights in real-world datasets.
bio.s1_sub: From interactive systems to data pipelines
bio.pivot_text: Game development taught me to think in systems, optimise under constraints, and iterate fast. When I started digging into player-behaviour datasets and performance telemetry, I realised data analysis gave me the same creative problem-solving loop — but at a scale that could drive real decisions. The leap felt natural.
bio.s2_sub: Shipped titles from my game development era
bio.p1_desc: End-to-end pipeline built on MotherDuck / DuckDB — cleaned 1.6M job postings, built 12 visualisations, and trained a salary regression model achieving R² 0.35 on US market data.
bio.p2_desc: Next data engineering project currently in progress. Stack and dataset TBD — check back soon.

p2.sub: The next data engineering project is currently in the works. Stack and dataset TBD — check back soon.
